2604.14362.title: APEX-MEM: Agentic Semi-Structured Memory with Temporal Reasoning for Long-Term Conversational AI
2604.14362.paper_summary: Large language models still struggle with reliable long-term conversational memory: simply enlarging context windows or applying naive retrieval often introduces noise and destabilizes responses. We present APEX-MEM, a conversational memory system that combines three key innovations: (1) a property graph which uses domain-agnostic ontology to structure conversations as temporally grounded events in an entity-centric framework, (2) append-only storage that preserves the full temporal evolution of information, and (3) a multi-tool retrieval agent that understands and resolves conflicting or evolving information at query time, producing a compact and contextually relevant memory summary. This retrieval-time resolution preserves the full interaction history while suppressing irrelevant details. APEX-MEM achieves 88.88% accuracy on LOCOMO's Question Answering task and 86.2% on LongMemEval, outperforming state-of-the-art session-aware approaches and demonstrating that structured property graphs enable more temporally coherent long-term conversational reasoning.
2604.14362.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在可靠的長期對話記憶方面仍然存在困難：僅僅擴大上下文窗口或應用簡單的檢索方法通常會引入噪音並使回應不穩定。我們提出了APEX-MEM，一個結合三個關鍵創新之對話記憶系統：（1）一個屬性圖，使用與領域無關的本體來將對話結構化為以時間為基礎的事件，並採用以實體為中心的框架，（2）僅附加存儲，保留信息的完整時間演變，以及（3）一個多工具檢索代理，能夠理解並解決查詢時的衝突或演變信息，生成一個緊湊且與上下文相關的記憶摘要。這種檢索時的解決方案保留了完整的互動歷史，同時抑制了不相關的細節。APEX-MEM在LOCOMO的問題回答任務中達到88.88%的準確率，在LongMemEval中達到86.2%，超越了最先進的會話感知方法，並證明結構化屬性圖能夠實現更具時間一致性的長期對話推理。
2604.14362.author: Pratyay Banerjee et.al.
2604.14362.authors: Pratyay Banerjee, Masud Moshtaghi, Shivashankar Subramanian, Amita Misra, Ankit Chadha
